meta.goal: Find Ni-X alloy that self-resists oxidation above +0.3V without carbon shell protection

oc20_benchmarks.name: Ni111_H
oc20_benchmarks.source: DFT literature — Ni binds H too strongly

search_space.description: Bare Ni-X alloy surfaces — no carbon shell. Focus on oxidation-resistant compositions.

dopant_type.source: [REASONING] GNoME: Ni8Mo, Ni8W stable. Literature: NiMo, NiFe, NiCo for HOR.

dopant_percent.source: [REASONING] Wider range for Hướng B — explore Ni-rich to X-rich. GNoME Ni8Mo=11%Mo, Ni8W=11%W.

surface_facet.source: [FACT] Standard low-index FCC surfaces. (111) most stable, (100)/(110) more reactive.

temperature_K.source: [FACT] AEMFC operates at 70C — same as Hướng A

critical_potential_V.source: [FACT] Ni oxidizes above +0.3V — Source: Cornell Ni@C paper

hor_activity.source: [REASONING] Sabatier: thermoneutral ΔG_H* ≈ 0 optimal for HOR

oxidation_resistance.source: [REASONING] PROXY — less negative O_ads = harder to oxidize surface. Threshold -1.5 eV estimated from Ni(111) O_ads ≈ -2.0 eV (want LESS negative = more resistant). ⚠️ PROXY, needs JDFTx validation.
oxidation_resistance.note: ⚠️ This is a PROXY metric. True oxidation potential requires JDFTx grand-canonical calculation in Phase 2.

bulk_stability.source: [FACT] Standard thermodynamic stability criterion. Materials on/below convex hull are stable.

screening.note: OC20 predicts BOTH H_ads (HOR) and O_ads (oxidation proxy) on bare alloy surface

simulation.note: MACE NOT used for NEB/MD in Hướng B (no shell). Used only as backup for O_ads if OC20 fails.

validation.note: CRITICAL for Hướng B: JDFTx checks if alloy stays metallic at +0.3V in KOH. This is the TRUE oxidation resistance test.

structure_generator.description: Generate bare Ni-X alloy FCC slab with specified facet. No graphene shell. Random dopant substitution throughout slab (not just surface).

knowledge_sources.synterra_note: SynTERRA recipes mostly OXIDES (NiFe2O4 etc). Not directly applicable for metallic alloy synthesis. Lab will need: arc melting, sputtering, or co-reduction.
knowledge_sources.notebook_gap: No DFT data for NiMo/NiW oxidation potential in papers. Proxy metric used. JDFTx Phase 2 will provide ground truth.
